# Issue #8771: Raise exception when user-specified reader package is not installed
**Repository:** Project-MONAI/MONAI
**Author:** haoyu-haoyu
**Status:** open
**Created At:** 2026-03-11T17:28:37Z

## Description
## Fixes #7437

### Description

When a user explicitly specifies a reader (e.g., `reader="ITKReader"`) but the required package is not installed, `LoadImage` now raises an `OptionalImportError` instead of silently falling back to another reader with only a warning.

**Before (current behavior):**
```python
# User specifies ITKReader but itk is not installed
loader = LoadImage(reader="ITKReader")
# Only a warning is printed, then PILReader is used silently
# This can lead to unexpected results
```

**After (new behavior):**
```python
# User specifies ITKReader but itk is not installed
loader = LoadImage(reader="ITKReader")
# OptionalImportError is raised immediately with a clear message:
# "Required package for reader ITKReader is not installed..."
```

**Default behavior (reader=None) remains unchanged** — missing optional packages are still handled gracefully with debug logging, and the system falls back to available readers as before.

### Changes

1. **`monai/transforms/io/array.py`**: Changed `warnings.warn()` to `raise OptionalImportError()` in the user-specified reader registration path (lines 212-216).

2. **`tests/transforms/test_load_image.py`**: Added `TestLoadImageReaderNotInstalled` test class with:
   - `test_specified_reader_not_installed_raises`: Direct test (runs when itk is NOT installed)
   - `test_specified_reader_not_installed_raises_mocked`: Mock-based test that always runs, simulating a reader whose package is not installed

### Testing

- [x] New unit tests added
- [x] Existing tests unaffected (default `reader=None` path unchanged)

Signed-off-by: haoyu-haoyu <haoyu-haoyu@users.noreply.github.com>

## Comments
### Comment by coderabbitai[bot] at 2026-03-11T17:29:00Z
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: summarize by coderabbit.ai -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

Added a mapping `_READER_INSTALL_HINTS` mapping reader names to pip install hints. LoadImage now, when registering a user-specified reader by name and an OptionalImportError occurs, appends an installation hint (when available) to the raised OptionalImportError instead of issuing a warning and falling back. Tests: new TestLoadImageReaderNotInstalled with two tests verifying LoadImage(reader=...) raises OptionalImportError with install hint (real and mocked missing readers). tests/data/test_init_reader.py updated to import OptionalImportError and wrap LoadImaged instantiation in try/except to skip assertions when a reader backend is missing.

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 2 (Simple) | ⏱️ ~10 minutes

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

### Comment by haoyu-haoyu at 2026-03-16T23:24:25Z
Updated with improved error messages:

**Before (v1):**
```
OptionalImportError: Required package for reader NibabelReader is not installed...
```
The user knows which reader failed but not which pip package to install.

**After (v2):**
```
OptionalImportError: required package `nibabel` is not installed or the version doesn't match requirement. Install with: pip install nibabel
```

Changes:
1. **Preserved the original error message** — `str(e)` already contains the package name (e.g. `nibabel`), so we prepend it instead of overwriting
2. **Added `_READER_INSTALL_HINTS` mapping** — reader name → pip install command for all built-in readers (NibabelReader, ITKReader, PILReader, PydicomReader, NrrdReader)
3. **Updated tests** to verify the install hint appears in the error and the original message is preserved

This also partially addresses #7980 (which requests install hints for writers) — the same pattern could be applied there.
### Comment by haoyu-haoyu at 2026-04-14T12:05:26Z
Friendly ping — this PR has been in `REVIEW_REQUIRED` status for about a month since the last push. Happy to rebase or address any concerns. The change is scoped to replacing the silent warning-and-fallback path with a clear `OptionalImportError` when a user explicitly passes `reader=...` and the underlying package is missing, plus a pip-install hint. @KumoLiu @ericspod would either of you have a moment to take a look?
### Comment by ericspod at 2026-04-14T13:56:43Z
> Friendly ping — this PR has been in `REVIEW_REQUIRED` status for about a month since the last push. Happy to rebase or address any concerns. The change is scoped to replacing the silent warning-and-fallback path with a clear `OptionalImportError` when a user explicitly passes `reader=...` and the underlying package is missing, plus a pip-install hint. @KumoLiu @ericspod would either of you have a moment to take a look?

Hi @haoyu-haoyu we have a number of other contributors wanting to resolve this issue which I've summarised [here](https://github.com/Project-MONAI/MONAI/issues/7437#issuecomment-4150922528). I had hoped to hear from the others to resolve which approach to take, we'd have to make a choice of them if not. Please have a look at the other PRs to see if any have addressed some detail not covered here.
### Comment by haoyu-haoyu at 2026-04-14T15:58:39Z
Thanks @ericspod — totally understand, and happy to collaborate on whichever approach lands.

Quick comparison of where the 5 PRs differ, in case it helps unblock the decision:

| PR | Exception type | Unique aspect |
|---|---|---|
| #8522 (@its-serah) | `OptionalImportError` | adds a new `raise_on_missing_reader` kwarg (opt-in) |
| #8768 (@abishop1990) | `RuntimeError` | friendlier wording |
| #8796 (@Talhax55z) | `OptionalImportError` | minimal change + test |
| #8823 (@KamayaniR) | `OptionalImportError` | test with mocked `ITKReader.__init__` |
| **#8771 (mine)** | `OptionalImportError` | **`_READER_INSTALL_HINTS` mapping → error message tells user which `pip install ...` to run** |

Four of the five PRs settle on `OptionalImportError` over `RuntimeError`, which matches the existing MONAI convention. The one distinctly useful thing mine carries that none of the others do is the pip-install hint in the error message (this also partially addresses the sibling issue #7980 for writer hints).

Three possible paths forward:

1. **Merge mine as-is** — it's the smallest change that gives users both "this is what failed" AND "here's how to fix it". Happy to rebase and address any specific comments.
2. **Combine** — land one of the simpler `OptionalImportError` PRs first, then I'll open a follow-up that just adds the `_READER_INSTALL_HINTS` layer on top.
3. **Land someone else's** — totally fine to close #8771 and let the others take it. I'll port the install-hint idea to a separate small PR if you'd like.

Let me know which you prefer. Either way, thanks for keeping everyone in the loop.
### Comment by its-serah at 2026-04-16T06:22:41Z
Hi @haoyu-haoyu , really appreciate the comparison, this helps clarify the tradeoffs a lot.

I agree that OptionalImportError is the right direction, and the _READER_INSTALL_HINTS is a solid addition, the pip hint definitely improves usability.

In my PR (#8522), I introduced raise_on_missing_reader mainly to preserve backward compatibility, since switching from silent fallback to raising could affect existing workflows.

That said, I think there’s a clean way to align:

keep the explicit error behavior (depending on maintainers’ preference: default vs opt-in)
integrate the install hint layer for better guidance

I’m happy to adapt my PR or collaborate on merging the approaches, whichever direction we decide to take 👍
